title: anthurium jicoteense
intro: Anthurium jicoteense, also known as the Guatemalan anthurium or Jicotea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arum. It is native to the tropical regions of Central America, particularly in Guatemala and Honduras. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The leaves and flowers of Anthurium jicoteense have been used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations.
